fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to indiana, speech-language pathologist compact indiana, indiana speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Board
board.mailing_address: Indiana Professional Licensing Agency, 402 W. Washington Street, Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204
board.physical_address: 402 W. Washington St., Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Indiana.

license_types.title: Temporary SLP Permit
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register for an account on the MyLicense Indiana portal and complete the online application for initial Speech-Language Pathologist licensure. Pay the required application fee through the portal.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts verifying a master's or doctoral degree from an ASHA-accredited program to be sent directly from your university to the Indiana Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Board.

steps.title: Submit Praxis Exam Scores
steps.description: Request the Educational Testing Service (ETS) to send your official Praxis exam (Speech-Language Pathology, code 5331 or current equivalent) scores directly to the Indiana Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Board. A minimum score of 162 is required.

steps.title: Submit Clinical Fellowship Report
steps.description: Have your supervisor complete and submit the 'Verification of Supervised Post-Graduate Professional Experience (Clinical Fellowship Report Form)' directly to the Board, detailing your completed clinical fellowship (36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time).

steps.title: Complete Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Access and pass the Indiana Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically administered online within your MyLicense Indiana account after application submission.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Indiana permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Indiana issues a Temporary SLP Permit for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Indiana is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Indiana through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does an Indiana SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The application fee is $50 — one of the lowest in the country. Biennial renewal is also $50.

faqs.question: Is Indiana part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indiana has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Indiana allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indiana permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

faqs.question: Does Indiana have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indiana participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Indiana without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Indiana is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Indiana through a compact privilege.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Indiana
hero.dek: To apply for an Indiana Speech-Language Pathologist license, you must create an account and submit your application through the official MyLicense Indiana online portal.

official_links.registration_steps: New applicants must register for an account on the MyLicense Indiana portal by clicking 'Register a Person'. After creating a username and password, you can log in and select 'Start a New Application' for Speech-Language Pathologist licensure.
